title: Quiz: Quantum Conditional Entropy
description: Test your understanding of quantum conditional entropy

questions.question: For a Bell state |Φ+⟩ = (|00⟩ + |11⟩)/√2, what is S(A|B)?
questions.explanation: For a Bell state: S(AB) = 0 (pure), S(B) = 1 (maximally mixed). So S(A|B) = 0 - 1 = -1. Negative conditional entropy indicates maximal entanglement.

questions.question: Classical conditional entropy H(X|Y) can be negative.
questions.explanation: False. Classical conditional entropy is always non-negative: H(X|Y) ≥ 0. Negative conditional entropy is a purely quantum phenomenon.

questions.question: Negative S(A|B) indicates:
questions.explanation: Negative conditional entropy is a signature of quantum entanglement. It cannot occur classically and indicates that A and B share quantum correlations.

questions.question: In state merging, negative S(A|B) means:

options.text: Alice must send qubits to Bob
EPR pairs are generated as a byproduct

questions.explanation: When S(A|B) < 0, state merging not only transfers Alice's state but also generates |S(A|B)| EPR pairs - entanglement is gained!

questions.question: For a product state ρ_AB = ρ_A ⊗ ρ_B, S(A|B) equals:
questions.explanation: For product states: S(AB) = S(A) + S(B). So S(A|B) = S(A) + S(B) - S(B) = S(A). Knowing B tells nothing about A.

questions.question: The range of S(A|B) for a state ρ_AB is:
questions.explanation: By Araki-Lieb: |S(A) - S(B)| ≤ S(AB), which implies -S(A) ≤ S(A|B) ≤ S(A). The range is symmetric around zero.

questions.question: Quantum mutual information I(A:B) can exceed min{S(A), S(B)}.
questions.explanation: True! For quantum states, I(A:B) = S(A) + S(B) - S(AB) can reach 2min{S(A),S(B)} for maximally entangled states, twice the classical maximum.

questions.question: Which statements are true about quantum conditional entropy? (Select all)
questions.explanation: a) Correct definition. b) FALSE - can be negative. c) Correct: S(AB) = 0 for pure, so S(A|B) = -S(B). d) FALSE: S(A|B) + S(B|A) = 2S(AB) - S(A) - S(B).
